steps.title: Translate the 4% annual increase
steps.content: Each year the population becomes the previous year's value plus 4% of it, so it is multiplied by $1 + 0.04 = 1.04$ every year.

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content:
Starting at $15{,}000$ when $x = 0$, after $x$ years the population is:
$$p = 15{,}000 \cdot (1.04)^x$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $p = 0.96(15{,}000)^x$: base $15{,}000$ is the starting value, not a growth factor — eliminate.
  • $p = 1.04(15{,}000)^x$: same problem — eliminate.
  • $p = 15{,}000(0.96)^x$: $0.96$ would mean a $4\%$ decrease — eliminate.
  • $p = 15{,}000(1.04)^x$: correct $4\%$ growth — matches.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
